State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance
(916) 322-5662

Administration • •
322-5660

Executive/Legal • •
322-5901

Enforcement • •
322-6441

Statements of Economic Interest
322-6444

January 31, 1984

Carter J. Stroud, Esq.
2807 Van Buren Street
Alameda, CA 94501

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-84-006

Dear Mr. Stroud:

Thank you for your letter of January 13, 1984, requesting
confirmation of telephone advice you received from this office.

You are presently the City Attorney of the City of Alameda.
The Municipal Court in the City of Alameda has the same
jurisdictional boundaries as the City. The Municipal Court
Judge is retiring, and you are planning to run for the
position. You asked whether any conflict of interest would
result from your running.

The Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000,
et seq., prohibits public officials from making or participating
in governmental decisions in which they have a financial
interest in the outcome. See Government Code Sections 87100 and
87101. The Act does not regulate the qualifications of persons
running for public office or otherwise speak to the propriety of
public officials running for particular positions. Accordingly,
there is no conflict under the Political Reform Act if you
choose to run for the Municipal Court seat.

There may be other laws or regulations which pertain to this
matter; I would specifically refer you to State Bar rules and
policies.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-6444.

Sincerely,

Diane Maura Fishourn
Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:pln

2806 Van Buren Street
Alameda, CA 94501
January 13, 1984


State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Fishbern:

I am the City Attorney of the City of Alameda.
The municipal court here has the same boundaries as
the City. The Judge is retiring and I am going to
file to run for his seat.

You indicated on the telephone that no conflict
would result from my running. Could I please have
that verified in writing?

Very truly yours,

Carter J. Stroud

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901        322-6441         322-6444

January 18, 1984


Carter J. Stroud
2806 Van Buren Street
Alameda, CA  94501                                Re: A-84-006

Dear Mr. Stroud:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Diane Maura Fishburn, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Bárbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel

BAM:plh
